opinion.h4: UPHAUS v. WYMAN, ATTORNEY GENERAL OF NEW HAMPSHIRE. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF NEW HAMPSHIRE. No. 332. Decided October 14, 1957.
opinion.p: Judgment vacated and cause remanded for consideration in the light of Sweezy v. New Hampshire, 354 U.S. 234.
Royal W. France and Leonard B. Boudin for appellant.
The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the Supreme Court of New Hampshire for consideration in light of Sweezy v. New Hampshire, 354 U.S. 234.
355 U.S. 16 LEWIS v. FLORIDA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF FLORIDA. No. 80, Misc. Decided October 14, 1957.
Richard W. Ervin, Attorney General of Florida, and David U. Tumin, Assistant Attorney General, for appellee.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
